title: Day 61: CIA Sovereignty Crisis — Mexico Sends Diplomatic Note Over Unauthorized CIA Drug Lab Raids; US-Mexico Counter-Narcotics Framework Under Strain
detail: Day 61 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). Fractura Total Day 13 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis internal war remains in a sustained buildup phase with no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the Jalisco heartland. 48 days to the FIFA World Cup 2026 opening match (June 11). DOMINANT DEVELOPMENT — CIA SOVEREIGNTY CRISIS: A major US-Mexico diplomatic rupture erupted after it was revealed that two U.S. CIA officers and two Mexican officials — including Román Oseguera Cervantes, director of the Chihuahua State Investigation Agency — died in a vehicle crash in the Sierra de Chihuahua on April 20, 2026, after conducting a counter-narcotics operation that destroyed six Sinaloa Cartel drug labs. The crash occurred in the rugged Sierra Tarahumara mountain highway connecting Chihuahua to Sinaloa. Mexico's President Claudia Sheinbaum stated on April 21–22 that her government had been 'unaware of any direct collaboration between the state of Chihuahua and personnel from the U.S. Embassy in Mexico,' and that such collaboration requires federal government authorization under Mexico's National Security Law. On April 22, the Foreign Relations Ministry sent a formal diplomatic note to the U.S. Embassy requesting an explanation for the unauthorized U.S. field operations, which Sheinbaum described as a potential violation of the National Security Law. Mexico's National Security Law states that foreign personnel may only engage in information-sharing activities — they are explicitly prohibited from conducting field operations, exercising powers reserved for Mexican authorities, or enforcing foreign laws on Mexican territory. SIGNIFICANCE FOR CJNG TRACKER: Although the CIA operation targeted Sinaloa Cartel drug labs (not CJNG), the sovereignty crisis is the most significant disruption to the US-Mexico counter-narcotics cooperation framework since El Mencho's killing. The bilateral intelligence-sharing and field-operations posture that enabled the Tapalpa operation (February 22) operates under the same legal framework now under challenge. Sheinbaum is weighing sanctions on Chihuahua state for collaborating with U.S. agents without federal authorization. The diplomatic note represents Mexico's most explicit public challenge to the scope of U.S. intelligence operations on its soil during the Sheinbaum administration. U.S. officials publicly confirmed the CIA affiliation of the two dead Americans. At stake is the operational framework that has driven 80+ Operativo Enjambre arrests, El Doble R's arrest (March 11), and the sustained post-Tapalpa pressure campaign against CJNG. García Harfuch confirmed the Security Ministry also had not been notified. DAY 61 SITUATIONAL STATUS: Fractura Total remains in buildup phase — dual faction mobilization without confirmed heartland combat. El Jardinero's 'Monstruos' deployment across Colotlán-Jalisco-Zacatecas border municipalities (April 18) continues to signal pre-combat positioning. O3 retains Grupo Élite command and Los Cuinis financial architecture. Next critical juncture: whether fractura total transitions to direct heartland combat before FIFA World Cup (48 days). Operativo Enjambre cumulative: 80+ officials detained. El 85 sentencing: July 31, 2026. ACLED 'After El Mencho' webinar recording now being processed for YouTube posting with English subtitles.

media.caption: Al Jazeera: Two CIA agents reportedly killed in car crash in Mexican state of Chihuahua after Sinaloa drug lab raid
CBS News: Mexico demands answers after CIA employees killed in car crash following drug lab raid — 'We were not informed'

title: Colombia-US Dismantle $190M CJNG-Linked Crypto Laundering Network — 5 Arrested Including Key Cartel Liaison 'Dans'
detail: On April 23, 2026, authorities in Colombia and the United States announced the simultaneous dismantlement of a money laundering network linked to CJNG that allegedly moved more than $190 million since 2023 through cryptocurrencies, shell companies, and couriers. The joint operation was carried out simultaneously in Bogotá, Cali, and Jamundí, Colombia, with at least seven raids and five arrests for extradition purposes. According to Dijín director Col. Elver Alfonso Sanabria, 'The group maintained direct ties with international cartels, specifically the CJNG, facilitating the entry of large sums of money into Colombia from cities such as Cleveland and Miami.' The investigation, which ran for more than six months, identified a hybrid money laundering scheme combining technological tools (cryptocurrency conversion) with traditional methods (bulk cash couriers). The network infiltrated Colombia's financial system through legally established businesses in the automotive and real estate sectors. KEY ARREST: Among those detained was Johan Alberto Gutiérrez Alonso, a Colombian-American citizen known as 'Dans' or 'Gordo,' identified as a key CJNG financial liaison. He coordinated drug trafficking to the United States and served as the primary interface between the Colombian laundering network and CJNG's financial operations. ASSET SEIZURES: Colombian authorities initiated asset forfeiture proceedings on 27 properties (movable and immovable) valued at more than $5 million. The Office of the Attorney General is advancing extradition procedures for all five suspects to the United States, where they face charges related to drug trafficking and money laundering. SIGNIFICANCE: This bust represents CJNG's expanding transnational financial architecture — the network moved $190M+ in just three years through the US and Colombia using crypto. Post-Mencho, CJNG's financial networks are under intense bilateral pressure; the fractura total between El Jardinero/El Sapo and O3/Los Cuinis has not disrupted CJNG's international money laundering infrastructure, which operates semi-autonomously from the succession struggle. The Colombia-US operation is coordinated through DEA and HSI channels, illustrating the multi-lateral counter-narcotics cooperation that complements Mexico's domestic Operativo Enjambre.

media.caption: UPI: Colombia, U.S. bust $190M crypto laundering network tied to CJNG — 5 arrests including key cartel liaison 'Dans'
